# Investment Committee Market Briefing

**Prepared for High-Quality Growth Stock Focus | U.S. Exchanges**

*Example briefing — replace by running the research pipeline.*

## I. MACRO ENVIRONMENT

### Federal Reserve & Rates
- Fed funds rate in the 4.25–4.50% range following a gradual easing cycle.
- Core PCE inflation tracking near 2.5%, giving the Fed room to hold or cut modestly.
- 10-year Treasury yields around 4.2–4.5%, keeping a modest cost-of-capital headwind on long-duration growth stocks.

### Growth
- U.S. GDP growth tracking 2.0–2.5% annualized. Labor market healthy, unemployment near 4.0–4.2%.
- Consumer spending bifurcated: resilient at the high end, softening for lower-income cohorts.

## II. SECTOR SNAPSHOT

| Sector | Trend | Key Driver |
|---|---|---|
| Technology / AI Infrastructure | Strong | AI capex supercycle; hyperscaler spending |
| Semiconductors | Strong (volatile) | AI tailwind vs. export restriction risk |
| Healthcare / Biotech | Selective | GLP-1 cycle; M&A activity |
| Consumer Discretionary | Bifurcated | Luxury softening; value outperforming |
| Consumer Staples | Underperforming | Expensive relative to bonds |

## III. COMMITTEE POSITIONING

### Overweight
- AI Infrastructure (data center, networking, hyperscalers)
- Semiconductors (with attention to export control risk)
- Healthcare Innovation (GLP-1 leaders)

### Underweight / Avoid
- High-multiple SaaS without clear FCF path
- China-revenue dependent names (trade policy risk)
- Traditional media / legacy retail
